reason: Adding the nosuid mount option to /home is a standard security hardening measure that mitigates the risk of privilege escalation via setuid/setgid executables in user home directories. It is unlikely to cause system breakage or lockout, as SUID/SGID permissions are not typically required in /home for standard operations.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential incompatibility with legacy applications that rely on setuid/setgid in /home; Requires remounting /home to apply changes, which may disrupt active sessions if not handled properly
